spec: Greenfield launch-content-pipeline Phase 2 (copy stage). src/copy.js exports verifyNumbers(text, spec) -> { ok, violations } and buildThread({ spec, generateHook }) -> string[]. A ContentSpec has shape { repoName, headline, metrics:[{name,value,scope}], scopeGuard, cta }. verifyNumbers must REJECT (ok:false) when EITHER (a) FABRICATED: text contains a number not present anywhere in the spec, or (b) UNGUARDED: a metric value appears in text but its scope string does not. Fully-guarded in-spec text returns ok:true. buildThread returns [hook, one-tweet-per-metric (value+scope inline), cta] and throws if the hook violates number-safety.
accept: every in-spec metric value stated WITH its scope guard is accepted (ok:true), INCLUDING multi-digit-run values like $0.32 whose digit-runs are 0 AND 32
reject: a number that is absent from the spec (fabricated, e.g. a '99 win rate') -> ok:false
an in-spec metric value stated WITHOUT its scope guard (unguarded) -> ok:false

example_table.input: verifyNumbers("$0.32 (n=27, dayjs/jest, 1 rep)", SPEC)  where SPEC has a metric { value: "$0.32", scope: "sonnet 1-shot, cheapest arm" } and another scope containing n=27
example_table.reference_output: ok:true (every number in the text is in the spec and guarded)
example_table.wrong_stub_output: ok:false (the wrong stub wrongly flags 32 as fabricated)
example_table.why: $0.32 has digit-runs 0 AND 32. An allowed-number set built with Set.add(...spread) keeps only the FIRST run per field (0) and forgets 32, so a later $0.32 in the text reads as a fabricated number.

eli5: The WRONG version builds its allowed-number list with Set.add(...spread), which only keeps the FIRST run of digits in each field. So for $0.32 it remembers 0 but forgets 32, then sees 32 in the text and screams 'fabricated!'. The RIGHT version loops and adds every run, so it knows both 0 and 32 and accepts $0.32. The agent test plants $0.32 with its scope guard and demands ok:true, so it goes RED against the wrong version and GREEN against the reference.
